dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic narrowly wins the day with the least-negative aggregate score, but this is a close call: its -0.026 score is only slightly ahead of Microsoft Copilot’s -0.043, and Copilot had just 6 mentions. Anthropic’s larger sample was mixed, with notable criticism around privacy/data collection [E5], safety reliability [E6], Claude Code performance [E7], and company hostility [E8], but it still compared better overall than OpenAI and Google Gemini, which faced much heavier negative sentiment.
winnerExplanation: Anthropic wins because it had the best aggregate sentiment score among the four entities on a reasonably large mention base: 426 mentions with a score of -0.0257. The result should be framed as a narrow, not decisive, win because Microsoft Copilot was close at -0.0431 but had only 6 mentions, making its score much less representative. Anthropic’s day was not strongly positive—evidence shows meaningful negative discussion about data/privacy practices, model safety, Claude Code benchmark performance, and hostility toward the company [E5][E6][E7][E8]. However, OpenAI and Google Gemini were substantially more negative, while Copilot’s near-tie was based on too small a sample to outweigh Anthropic’s broader aggregate performance.

openai.judgementSnippet: OpenAI had the weakest score of the day at -0.571 across 171 mentions, driven by strong negative discussion around legal/copyright allegations involving ChatGPT [E1], criticism of company strategy and resource commitments [E2][E4], and safety concerns about GPT-class models on harmful-action tasks [E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Anthropic was the daily winner, but only narrowly and with mixed-to-negative sentiment overall. Its score of -0.026 across 426 mentions was the best aggregate result, despite negative drivers around privacy/data collection [E5], harmful-action safety concerns [E6], Claude Code performance criticism [E7], and a hostile story about banning Anthropic employees [E8].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Google Gemini was clearly negative at -0.411 across 86 mentions. The strongest negative drivers involved a Gemini Live incident tied to family Google account bans [E9][E10] and Gemini CLI usability/latency complaints [E11]. There was some offsetting praise for Gemini Pro versus Claude [E12], but not enough to make the overall sentiment competitive.

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Microsoft Copilot finished close to Anthropic numerically at -0.043, but with only 6 mentions, so its result is low-sample and should not be overread. Discussion included one positive large-scale platform/product framing [E13], alongside mild negatives about Copilot being comparatively worse than Anthropic’s Opus 4.6 [E14], broader Microsoft criticism [E15], and Copilot backlash or controversial announcements [E16].

evidence.summary: The story frames OpenAI as the subject of a lawsuit over an alleged unauthorized ChatGPT version of a children's book, which is a clear negative legal/copyright issue for OpenAI.
The item explicitly criticizes OpenAI’s company strategy, alleging it reserved a huge share of global RAM without committing to purchase it.
The post says popular AI models including GPT scored very low on harmful-action safety and implies they lack barriers and are not reliable for critical projects.
The comment directly criticizes OpenAI's strategy as spin and bullshit and frames it as part of a larger bubble, implying strong disapproval of the company’s actions.

The story frames Anthropic as collecting too much user data, calling it a privacy nightmare and saying it can "hoover up about you." This is a direct negative judgment of the company’s data practices and strategy.
The post says popular AI models including Claude scored very low on harmful-action safety and implies they are not reliable enough for critical use.

The story headline directly frames Claude Code, an Anthropic product, as performing poorly by saying it "ranks 39th on terminal bench" and that the leaked source explains why. This is a direct negative judgment about Anthropic-linked model/product quality.
The story title describes banning all Anthropic employees, which is directly hostile toward Anthropic as a company and its staff.

The story directly ties Gemini Live to a severe negative outcome: the user's behavior in Gemini Live allegedly led to the entire family's Google accounts being banned. This is a clear negative judgment of the Google Gemini product/service experience.

The comment condemns Google for banning the family’s accounts after the Gemini Live incident and says Google should not be able to do this to them, framing it as an abusive company decision affecting access to their own data.
The comment criticizes the Gemini CLI experience as "not user-friendly" and says it "takes an extremely long time to wait," which is a strong negative UX complaint about Gemini.
Explicitly praises Gemini Pro as better than Claude and calls Google the only competent model provider in this comparison.

The comment describes building the platform behind Copilot and frames it as a large-scale, high-impact AI product reaching hundreds of millions of users. This is promotional/hiring language rather than critique.

Copilot is listed alongside ChatGPT as a product companies may be stuck with due to procurement cycles, contrasted with the claim that Opus 4.6 is currently better. That is mild negative comparative sentiment toward Copilot.

The comment compares Microsoft's likely response to Google's and implies Microsoft would behave the same way, which carries a mild critical tone toward Microsoft/Copilot's company strategy rather than product quality.
Mentions Copilot backlash and frames its product announcements as controversial, which is a mild negative judgment of the Copilot product experience.
